title: Prevention and cessation  quitting resources for youth - Canada.ca
content:
## Prevention and quitting resources for youth
- Vaping product safety
- Vaping and quitting smoking
- Prevention and quitting resources for youth
- Sharing information about vaping prevention and quitting
- Consider the consequences of vaping: Online self-led module
- Posts to share on social media
- Digital banners for your website and newsletters

## Sharing information about vaping prevention and quitting
Our resources include posters, handouts, videos and social media images that you can use to increase awareness about the harms of vaping and nicotine for youth.
These resources can help youth in Canada make informed choices about the risks of vaping and provide information to help those who want to quit.
Help us share these resources:
- on websites, through newsletters and emails
- within clinical practices
- within community groups or centers

## Consider the consequences of vaping: Online self-led module
Use the self-led vaping education module in your classroom or community settings to inform teens on the harms and risks associated with youth vaping. This module includes engaging activities, informative content, and Q&A examples.
Online self-led module: Educator resource
Schools, Community Groups/Centers

## Posts to share on social media
You can share these posts through your social media channels.
I quit for me guide - Youth guide
It may feel difficult, but quitting is possible! The "I quit for me" guide can help 14-18 year olds who want to quit using tobacco, cigarettes, or vapes. Learn more here: I quit for me #parents #teachers
It may feel difficult, but quitting is possible! The "I quit for me" guide can help 14-18 year olds who want to quit using tobacco, cigarettes, or vapes. Learn more here: I quit for me

I quit for me guide - Facilitator's guide
#Educators play an important role setting up #youth for success, in and out of school. Motivate and empower teens who smoke or vape to quit by using the "I quit for me" group program. Learn more: I quit for me: Facilitator’s guide for a group program

Consider the consequences of vaping
Children and youth are especially susceptible to the harmful effects of #nicotine, including addiction. They may become dependent on nicotine with lower levels of exposure than adults. Learn more: Canada.ca/vaping-info
Children and youth are especially susceptible to the harmful effects of #nicotine, including addiction. They may become dependent on nicotine with lower levels of exposure than adults.

Talk with your teen about vaping
#Vaping. #Nicotine. #Addiction. Are any of these words used in your home? Learning about the risks of vaping can help you answer questions from your kids. Check out our tip sheet for parents and start the conversation today. Talking with your teen about vaping: A tip sheet for parents
#Vaping. #Nicotine. #Addiction. Are any of these words used in your home? Learning about the risks of vaping can help you answer questions from your kids.
Check out our tip sheet for parents and start the conversation today. Talking with your teen about vaping: A tip sheet for parents

Vaping can expose you to harmful chemicals
Metal particles like nickel and lead can be found in vapes. You don't want any of these in your lungs. Keep your lungs healthy. Leave the vape behind. Learn more about the health risks of vaping for youth: Consider the Consequences of Vaping
Metal particles like nickel and lead can be found in vapes. You don't want any of these in your lungs.
Keep your lungs healthy. Leave the vape behind.
Learn more about the health risks of vaping for youth: Consider the Consequences of Vaping

Vaping nicotine can harm healthy teen brain development
It can make it harder for you to concentrate, learn and manage your emotions. Protect your brain. Leave the vape behind. Canada.ca/vaping-info
It can make it harder for you to concentrate, learn and manage your emotions.
